title: Snack Stalls in Sight
statement_md:
The tech fest food lane has `N` snack stalls standing in a single row, numbered `1` to `N` from the entrance. Stall `i` sells one snack type, marked by a code `S_i` — two different stalls can absolutely sell the same type (momos are everywhere).

A group doing the fest walk can see exactly `K` consecutive stalls at a time: first stalls `1..K`, then `2..K+1`, and so on, till the last stretch `N−K+1..N`.

For every position of the walk, from left to right, tell the group how many **distinct** snack types are visible in that stretch of `K` stalls.
input_format_md:
The first line contains a single integer `T` — the number of test cases.

For each test case:
- the first line contains two integers `N` and `K`
- the second line contains `N` integers `S_1 … S_N` — the snack type codes, in row order
output_format_md: For each test case, print **exactly one line** containing `N − K + 1` integers separated by single spaces — the number of distinct snack types in each window, from the leftmost window to the rightmost.
constraints_md:
- `1 ≤ T ≤ 10`
- `1 ≤ K ≤ N ≤ 2 × 10^4`
- the sum of `N` over all test cases is at most `4 × 10^4`
- `1 ≤ S_i ≤ 10^9`
hints: Recounting every window from scratch repeats almost all the work — when the window slides one step, only one stall enters and one stall leaves.
Keep a hash map of counts for the types currently in the window. When a type's count goes from 0 to 1 the distinct count goes up by one; when it drops from 1 to 0 it goes down by one.

reference_solution.python:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    pos = 0
    t = int(data[pos]); pos += 1
    out = []
    for _ in range(t):
        n, k = int(data[pos]), int(data[pos + 1]); pos += 2
        s = data[pos:pos + n]; pos += n  # bytes tokens are hashable as-is
        freq = {}
        distinct = 0
        res = []
        for i in range(n):
            x = s[i]
            c = freq.get(x, 0)
            if c == 0:
                distinct += 1
            freq[x] = c + 1
            if i >= k:
                y = s[i - k]
                c2 = freq[y] - 1
                freq[y] = c2
                if c2 == 0:
                    distinct -= 1
            if i >= k - 1:
                res.append(distinct)
        out.append(" ".join(map(str, res)))
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out) + "\n")

main()
